publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-04-29 23:21:40 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Register containing parishioners, baptisms, confirmations, mariages, communicants, burials and church offerings. Also included is a certificate of incorporation (1841).

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
bioghist.p: Christ Church of Corning was legally incorporated on April 12, 1841. The congregation was first served by the Reverend Richard Smith, a missionary, who also held Episcopal services at Painted Post. Services were held in the schoolhouse of District 14 and special ceremonies (marriages, communions, etc.) were held in the Methodist church in Corning until a chapel was built sometime before 1850. A stone church building was begun in 1853, and consecrated by the Right Reverend William H. DeLancey, Bishop of the Western New York Diocese on September 21, 1854. Bishop DeLancey visited the Corning parish several times during the 1850's and his visits are recorded in the parish register.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: The certificate of incorporation (1841) is also transcribed in the register. The register (1841-1869) records parishioners, baptisms, confirmations, marriages, communicants, burials and church offerings in semi-chronological order. The register is paginated but there are gaps in page numbers between each section. Some tipped-in notes at the back of the register date to 1895.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Christ Church of Corning records, #6077. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.geogname: Steuben County (N.Y.) -- Religious life and customs.
controlaccess.subject: Episcopal Church -- New York (State) -- Corning.
Church records and registers -- New York (State) -- Corning.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed by the church clerk. The names of church wardens and vestrymen are included.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Note: Numbered pages begin on page 22, and there are gaps in page numbers between pp. 35-48, 150-191, 196-239, 250-257, 264-305, and 323-328.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Parish organized April 1841. Rev'd Richard Smith, Missionary." This is a brief history, listing year, number of families, adults, children, baptisms, and communicants in the parish, 1841-1869.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists year, number confirmed, buried; number of public services held; number of communicants, teachers and students in the Sunday School, number of marriages; funds collected for diocesan missions, domestic missions, foreign missions, Christmas fund, tracts, alms, parish objects, Thanksgiving Day offering; Rector/ministers' names.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: written history of incorporation (June 9, 1841, signed by church clerk). The certificate of incorporation is transcribed here.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists dates, names, residence, hen removed, married, deceased, remarks. Note: numbered pages begin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A family joins the Methodist church "during the excitement of the winter" of 1857-1858.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Newspaper clipping, tipped-in (n.d.) re: first labor day parade in Corning.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Number of parishioners reported to the Diocese, August 1867.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Records date, name, parents, sponsors, birth date, officiating clergyman and remarks, where performed.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Adult baptisms (1 or 2 pages only).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three short entries regarding visits and baptisms by Bishop DeLancey in Corning.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Records date, name, officiating bishop, remarks, whether communed. Remarks are extensive descriptions of the ceremonies .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists date, name, residence, dismissals (and other remarks).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists date, names, place, officiating clergyman and witness name.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Records date, name, place of interment, age.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Records date, object, remarks, amount, p. 317 One sheet paper, tipped in, notes "parochial items" from August 1, 1868 to December 13, 1868.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter, tipped in, requesting that the Christ Church of Coming's rector provide proof of the marriage of a recent widow; also a newspaper obituary, n.d.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Chancel," tipped in paper: format of church and pews; records rentals for 1878 and 1885(7). On other side are notes of 2 baptisms (1860 and 1861), one death (1873), and other illegible notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Parish Expenses," tipped in note, 1854. Records balance due to rector(?) More of the same on other side.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet (1895) tipped in, re: 10th anniversary of the chancel building of Christ's Church, Easter Day, 1895.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Record of "disbursement of alms," n.d., 1/2 page only.
